title: Product Marketing Alignment
summary: The meeting covered developer plugins, internal account issues, third-party security concerns (specifically a Trojan in Axios), and website restructuring project updates. Matt detailed a personal automation project aimed at scaling to an organizational-level conversational CRM, and the team discussed agent architecture and orchestration for efficient data processing.

decisions.description: Matt will pass on the warning about the Axios Trojan to their development channels.
Matt will check with Herenda for updates on the team account and premium access.
Paulo will check with Sergey regarding Snowflake access unblocking.
The new website restructuring project will hydrate from a content lake powered by a Groq database, processing around 120 articles clustered by topics like BDC performance and lead management.
Migrate current Docker-intensive local development over to a Mac Mini to run the processes locally.

actionItems.task: Implement well-defined agents that can evaluate nuances before any auto-response or automation occurs.

expectedOutcome.description: A personal OS side project that ingests signals from multiple sources to categorize and automate actions, intended to scale to an organizational-level conversational CRM.

actionItems.task: Define agents with specific jobs so that tasks are executed as desired, with appropriate tooling and restricted access.

expectedOutcome.description: Orchestrator will spawn parallel sub-agents to perform specific tasks, which are ephemeral workers and can communicate, but not spawn further sub-agents.

tensions: Confusion about trusting large models like Sonnet or Opus to architect the agent system correctly during a brainstorming session.
Paulo questioned the monetization strategy for the Conserver given that the technology is open source.
